story.iWant: three representative reference images (Portrait, Landscape, Product) to preview presets on
story.soThat: I can see how a preset affects different photography genres before converting my own files
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Select Reference Images (AC-1, AC-3, AC-5) - Portrait image: Neutral skin tones, natural lighting, soft background - Landscape image: Blue sky, green foliage, good tonal range - Product image: Neutral background, varied colors, even lighting - Source: Unsplash, Pexels, Pixabay (CC0 license verification) - High resolution (2000px+ width for downscaling) ### Task 2: Optimize Images for Web (AC-2) - Install optimization tools (cwebp, ImageMagick) - Resize images to target dimensions: - Desktop: 1200px width - Tablet: 800px width - Mobile: 400px width - Convert to WebP format (quality 80) - Create JPEG fallbacks (quality 85) - Verify file sizes (<200 KB each, total <600 KB) - Adjust quality if needed to meet file size targets
### Task 3: Create Image Directory Structure (AC-4) - Create `web/images/` directory - Move optimized images to directory - Verify directory structure with all image variants
### Task 4: Create Attribution File (AC-3) - Create `web/images/CREDITS.md` with photographer attribution - Document image sources, licenses, original URLs - Verify CC0 licenses for all images
### Task 5: Implement Responsive Image HTML (AC-6) - Add `` elements to preview modal HTML - Implement responsive srcset attributes (400w, 800w, 1200px) - Add JavaScript to switch between reference images - Support lazy loading with loading="lazy" attribute ### Task 6: Optional Preload Optimization (AC-4) - Add `` for portrait image (default) - Test impact on page load time - Keep preload if improvement >200ms, remove if negligible ### Task 7: Visual Quality Validation (AC-7) - Test images at full size (no pixelation, no artifacts) - Test images with CSS filters (Story 11.1 integration) - Test images in before/after slider (Story 11.4 integration) ### Task 8: Browser Compatibility Testing (AC-7) - Test WebP support on all browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) - Test JPEG fallback on older browsers - Test responsive images on mobile devices ### Task 9: Performance Testing (AC-2, AC-6) - Measure image load times (desktop, tablet, mobile) - Lighthouse audit for image optimization - Network waterfall analysis for lazy loading verification ### Task 10: Documentation (AC-1, AC-3) - Add reference images section to README - Document image optimization process for contributors

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**AC-1: Three Reference Images Provided** - Portrait reference image (person, natural lighting, skin tones) - Landscape reference image (outdoor scene, blue sky, greenery) - Product/Still-life reference image (inanimate object, studio lighting) - Images representative of 80%+ typical preset use cases - Neutral starting point (no extreme colors or lighting) **AC-2: Image Optimization for Web** - File format: WebP primary, JPEG fallback - File size: <200 KB each (Portrait <150KB, Landscape <180KB, Product <120KB) - Total bundle: <600 KB - Image dimensions: 1200px width (desktop), variable height maintaining aspect ratio - Aspect ratios: Portrait 3:4/2:3 vertical, Landscape 16:9/3:2 horizontal, Product 4:3/1:1 square - Compression: WebP quality 80-85%, JPEG quality 85-90% - Lossless optimization (remove EXIF metadata, color profiles)
**AC-3: Licensing and Attribution** - Licensing: Public domain (CC0) or created for Recipe - No copyright restrictions, no attribution required, commercial use allowed - Sources: Unsplash/Pexels/Pixabay (CC0 verified) or Justin's photos - Attribution file: `web/images/CREDITS.md` with sources, photographers, licenses - No licensing conflicts (no BY-SA, ND, NC restrictions)
**AC-4: Images Embedded in Web Bundle** - Images stored in `web/images/` directory: - preview-portrait.webp, preview-landscape.webp, preview-product.webp - Fallback JPEGs: preview-portrait.jpg, preview-landscape.jpg, preview-product.jpg - No external requests (self-hosted, no CDN) - Optional preload for performance: `` for portrait (default) **AC-5: Images Work Well with Typical Presets** - Neutral starting point (not over-saturated, neutral exposure, good tonal range) - Test with typical adjustments: Exposure ±1.0, Contrast ±0.5, Saturation ±0.5, Hue ±30°, Temperature ±20 - Images representative of real-world usage (skin tones, sky/foliage, neutral backgrounds) **AC-6: Responsive Image Handling** - Responsive sizes: Desktop 1200px, Tablet 800px, Mobile 400px - `` element with srcset for multiple sizes - Lazy loading: loading="lazy" attribute - Total bundle: Desktop 600KB, Tablet 400KB, Mobile 200KB **AC-7: Image Selection and Validation** - Image selection criteria documented (skin tones, outdoor colors, neutral backgrounds) - Visual validation (full size, CSS filters applied, before/after slider) - Technical validation (dimensions, file sizes, format, browser loading)

docs.doc: Epic 11 tech spec defines reference image requirements: Three images (Portrait, Landscape, Product) optimized for web (<200KB each, total <600KB). Images embedded in web bundle (no external requests). Licensing must be public domain or created for Recipe. Responsive sizes (1200px, 800px, 400px). WebP format with JPEG fallback.

Path A PRD defines preview system scope for Phase 1. FR-4.2 (Reference Image Bundle) requires three bundled reference images optimized for web, representative of common photography genres, embedded in web bundle (no external requests), public domain licensing.

Story 11.1 implements CSS filter preview logic that will be applied to reference images from Story 11.2. Maps Recipe parameters to CSS filter functions (brightness, contrast, saturate, hue-rotate). Reference images must work well with these CSS filter approximations.

Story 11.3 creates preview modal structure that displays reference images. Modal includes tabs (Portrait/Landscape/Product), before/after slider, parameter display. Story 11.2 provides the actual image assets that Story 11.3 displays.

Story 11.4 implements interactive slider showing before/after comparison on reference images. Reference images from Story 11.2 must work well in slider context (clear visual differences when CSS filters applied).
Story 11.5 adds disclaimer explaining CSS filter limitations. Reference images help users understand preview accuracy by showing approximate vs. actual conversion differences.

Browser compatibility documentation lists Web API support. WebP images supported in Chrome 32+, Firefox 65+, Safari 14+, Edge 18+. JPEG fallback required for older browsers. `` element with srcset for responsive images supported in all modern browsers.

README documents supported browsers and required features. WebAssembly, File API, Blob API, Drag-and-drop events, CSS Custom Properties, Flexbox/Grid Layout all supported. Browser support: Chrome 57+, Firefox 52+, Safari 11+, Edge 16+.

artifacts.code: <artifact path="web/static/style.css" kind="css" lines="1-100" symbol="CSS styling" reason="Main CSS file for web interface styling. Includes responsive design breakpoints (mobile <768px, tablet 768-1024px, desktop >1024px). Reference images will use responsive CSS for different viewport sizes." />

dependencies.dependency: Story 11.2 has zero external dependencies. Reference images are static assets created or sourced with CC0 license. No frameworks, no libraries, no external image CDNs.
Modern image format with better compression than JPEG. Supported in Chrome 32+, Firefox 65+, Safari 14+, Edge 18+. JPEG fallback required for older browsers.
Responsive images with multiple sources (srcset). Supported in all modern browsers. Allows serving different image sizes based on viewport width.
Image processing tool for resizing, converting, and optimizing images. Used during image preparation (not runtime dependency). Available on macOS (brew), Windows (download), Linux (apt/yum).
WebP compression tool for converting JPEG/PNG to WebP format. Used during image preparation (not runtime dependency). Available via package managers or Google Downloads.

constraints.constraint: Image file sizes MUST be <200 KB each (AC-2). Total bundle <600 KB to meet 3G load time target (<2 seconds). Requires aggressive WebP compression (quality 75-85%) and removal of EXIF metadata.
Licensing MUST be CC0 (public domain) or created for Recipe (AC-3). No restrictive licenses (BY-SA, ND, NC) allowed. All images must support commercial use without attribution requirements.
Images MUST be embedded in web bundle (AC-4). No external CDN requests, no image hosting services. All images served from Recipe domain for privacy-first architecture.

Images MUST work well with CSS filters (AC-5). Neutral starting point required (not over-saturated, neutral exposure). Must show clear differences when CSS filters applied (exposure, contrast, saturation, hue adjustments).
Responsive image sizes MUST match breakpoints (AC-6). Desktop 1200px, Tablet 800px, Mobile 400px align with responsive design from Story 2-10. `` element with srcset required for proper sizing.
Image optimization MUST be lossless where possible (AC-2). Remove EXIF metadata, remove color profiles, optimize compression. Balance file size vs. visual quality (WebP quality 80 target).
Image selection MUST represent common photography genres (AC-1). Portrait (skin tones), Landscape (outdoor colors), Product (neutral backgrounds) cover 80%+ typical preset use cases.

Browser compatibility MUST support WebP with JPEG fallback (AC-7). WebP for modern browsers (Chrome 32+, Firefox 65+, Safari 14+), JPEG fallback for older browsers. Test on real devices (iPhone, Android, desktop).

interface.name: Preview Modal DOM Structure (Story 11.3)
interface.signature: modal with tabs, slider, reference images

interfaces.interface: Story 11.3 creates preview modal where reference images will be displayed. Modal includes tabs (Portrait/Landscape/Product) for switching between reference images, before/after slider showing original vs. filtered images, parameter display showing preset adjustments. Story 11.2 provides the actual image assets (preview-portrait.webp, preview-landscape.webp, preview-product.webp) that Story 11.3 displays.

Story 11.1 implements CSS filter mapping function that converts UniversalRecipe parameters to CSS filter string. Reference images from Story 11.2 must work well with these filters. Function maps: Exposure → brightness(), Contrast → contrast(), Saturation → saturate(), Hue → hue-rotate(), Temperature/Tint → sepia() + hue-rotate() approximation.

Story 2-10 implements responsive design with breakpoints: Mobile <768px, Tablet 768-1024px, Desktop>1024px. Reference images use responsive srcset to serve appropriate sizes: 400px (mobile), 800px (tablet), 1200px (desktop). Touch detection logic reused for mobile image interactions.

Story 11.4 implements interactive slider for before/after comparison. Reference images displayed twice (before and after layers) with CSS clip-path revealing filtered version based on slider position. Slider handle (range input) updates clip-path percentage. Keyboard accessible (arrow keys), touch-friendly on mobile.

Story 2-2 implements file upload interface with drag-drop zone and file picker fallback. After file uploaded and format detected, "Preview" button triggers modal showing reference images with preset adjustments applied. Integration point: showPreview(file, recipe) function in preview.js.

tests.standards: Recipe testing standards for Epic 11 stories: - Manual testing required (Story 11.2 is asset preparation, no unit tests) - Image optimization validation: File sizes, dimensions, format, visual quality - Browser compatibility testing: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (WebP + JPEG fallback) - Mobile device testing: iPhone (Safari), Android (Chrome), responsive breakpoints - Visual validation: Full-size quality, CSS filter compatibility, before/after slider rendering - Performance testing: Lighthouse audit, 3G connection load times, lazy loading verification - Documentation validation: README updated, CREDITS.md complete, contributor guide accurate
tests.locations: - Image source selection: Unsplash (https://unsplash.com), Pexels (https://pexels.com), Pixabay (https://pixabay.com) - Image optimization tools: ImageMagick (convert command), Google WebP Tools (cwebp command) - Image storage: web/images/ directory (preview-portrait.webp/jpg, preview-landscape.webp/jpg, preview-product.webp/jpg) - Attribution documentation: web/images/CREDITS.md - Browser testing: Chrome DevTools (F12), Firefox DevTools, Safari Web Inspector - Mobile device testing: iPhone SE (smallest modern screen), Android mid-range, iPad - Performance testing: Lighthouse CI, WebPageTest (3G connection simulation)
tests.ideas:
**AC-1 Testing (Three Reference Images):** - Portrait validation: Person visible, natural lighting, skin tones identifiable, neutral background, works with typical presets - Landscape validation: Outdoor scene, blue sky visible, greenery visible, good tonal range (shadows/midtones/highlights), neutral exposure - Product validation: Inanimate object, studio/controlled lighting, neutral background (white/gray/black), varied colors for saturation testing - Genre coverage: Verify images represent 80%+ typical preset use cases (skin tone adjustments, landscape color grading, product enhancement) **AC-2 Testing (Image Optimization):** - File size validation: Portrait <150KB, Landscape <180KB, Product <120KB, Total <600KB - Dimension validation: Desktop 1200px width, Tablet 800px width, Mobile 400px width - Format validation: WebP primary (quality 80-85%), JPEG fallback (quality 85-90%) - Compression validation: Visual quality acceptable (no pixelation, no artifacts at 100% zoom) - EXIF removal: Verify metadata stripped (no privacy concerns, smaller file size)
**AC-3 Testing (Licensing):** - License verification: Unsplash/Pexels/Pixabay CC0 label confirmed on source page - CREDITS.md validation: All images documented with photographer, source, license, original URL - Commercial use: Verify no restrictive licenses (no BY-SA, ND, NC) - Attribution requirement: Verify CC0 (no attribution required)
**AC-4 Testing (Web Bundle Embedding):** - Directory structure: Verify web/images/ contains all image variants (3 WebP + 3 JPEG + responsive sizes) - No external requests: Network tab shows all images loaded from local domain (no CDN, no external URLs) - Preload optimization: Portrait image preloaded on page load (optional), verify load time improvement>200ms **AC-5 Testing (CSS Filter Compatibility):** - Neutral baseline: Images not over-saturated, neutral exposure, good tonal range - Exposure ±1.0: Apply brightness(150%) and brightness(50%), verify no clipping/crushing - Contrast ±0.5: Apply contrast(150%) and contrast(50%), verify improved/reduced separation - Saturation ±0.5: Apply saturate(150%) and saturate(50%), verify colorful/desaturated result - Hue ±30°: Apply hue-rotate(30deg) and hue-rotate(-30deg), verify subtle color shift - Temperature ±20: Apply sepia() + hue-rotate() approximation, verify warm/cool result **AC-6 Testing (Responsive Images):** - Desktop (1200px): Verify 1200px WebP loaded, file size ~150KB-180KB - Tablet (800px): Verify 800px WebP loaded, file size ~80KB-100KB - Mobile (400px): Verify 400px WebP loaded, file size ~30KB-40KB - `` element: Verify srcset attributes correct, media queries match breakpoints - Lazy loading: Verify loading="lazy" attribute present, images load only when modal opened **AC-7 Testing (Visual Validation):** - Full-size quality: Open each image at 100% zoom, check for pixelation/artifacts - CSS filter rendering: Apply filters, verify images still look good (not oversaturated/clipped) - Before/after slider: Drag slider 0-100%, verify smooth transition, clear comparison - Browser compatibility: Chrome (WebP), Firefox (WebP), Safari (WebP), Edge (WebP), Safari 13 (JPEG fallback) - Mobile devices: iPhone (Safari), Android (Chrome), iPad (Safari) - verify correct sizes loaded **Performance Testing:** - 3G load time: Measure total load time on WebPageTest (3G connection), target <2 seconds - Lighthouse audit: Run Lighthouse, verify "Serve images in next-gen formats" passes, "Properly size images" passes, "Defer offscreen images" passes - Network waterfall: DevTools Network tab, verify images load only when modal opened (lazy loading)
**Edge Cases:** - JPEG fallback: Test on Safari 13 or older, verify JPEG loads correctly - Extremely small screens (<320px): Verify images scale down correctly, remain readable - Slow connections (2G): Verify images load eventually, no broken image icons - Orientation change (mobile): Portrait → Landscape, verify images reflow correctly
